content:
Inevitable I'd make a mistake like this, ironic that it's the *"it's always Temp. STR▼"* thing that I've been double-checking since I got it wrong the first three times LOL

the Posses were actually one of the only things (Orisons being the only other one) that I didn't bother to let the community help proofread for, so to me this is confirmation that I definitely should be doing that.

I will definitely continue to do the info gathering, evidence collecting, analytical and QC/playtesting work that I've been doing, and report back with everything I find that could be of value, even if I'll be hands off of the source files for a while!

As soon as all my free time isn't being absorbed by playing goofy teams in the raid event, I'll set my sights on making my Coordination repo ready for a 2nd wave of volunteers, which should be relatively easy if I don't have to break down the gameplay sheet <:aurita_dizzy:1408324284702654556>
Yeah I'm so sorry we're like this
Just now I asked Mijiu "so is Card Lock and Action Lock the same thing"
he was like
"what is that"
*Mijiu is the lead combat designer
Card Lock? Action Lock?
the Traphase mechanism
Oh
Daffodil does it
Pvp shit
LOL
also apparently it's the same thing
they forgot they used 2 terms for the same thing, uuuuhhh fixing rn
Oof
i also just showed them the places where chinese lacked a proper tag or used the wrong tag and you helped us fix
Yay! I know I kinda forgot to keep taking notes... But I have a list still <:aurita_dizzy:1408324284702654556>
so like this is why i finally thought we're probably not ready for volunteer direct translation input on gameplay stuff rn<:lmfao:1408867314497228941>
i hope we will be later
it's good they're at least starting to fix
I can send it to you if you want, but you probably already saw it all if you're looking thru my work
The notes I have are pretty much only from Posses I think 😅
sure! can we have your note?
bonking mijiu head with it
Yeah! I'll forward it to you

Hi! A heads up: 

In this Posse retranslation submitted last month there was a mistranlation, where Doresain's Posse was mistranslated into gaining Permanent STR instead of Temporary STR.

Sorry we did not notice this issue during the screening before importing volunteer contribution. The text in-game is currently wrong as a result.

We will fix it in the latest hotfix tomorrow.<:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541>  https://github.com/omnichromia/Morimens-Localizations/blob/d858defe9e5a264abbdcceea5d08890a5e98e67a/TRANSLATIONS/01_UPLOAD_YOUR_CONTRIBUTIONS/en/EN_Posse_Translation.csv

embeds.title: Morimens-Localizations/TRANSLATIONS/01_UPLOAD_YOUR_CONTRIBUTIONS/en...
embeds.description: Contribute to omnichromia/Morimens-Localizations development by creating an account on GitHub.

content:
oh Ace! speaking of wrong descriptions, is it possible to update this description as well since it's currently wrong ingame?
https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1445108630197375170/1445301255629967360
yeah, Light just told the combat designer to probably change up the posse description as a whole in Chinese yesterday
or even "redesign" it, whatever he meant by that... we'll update the translation accordingly when it happens!

Again, I must say thank you so much for all the incredible work you put into helping us standardize our skills and improve the quality of the English text. 

We especially want to call out volunteers like Mercy, whose insightful suggestions have been a huge learning experience for our combat and design teams. We truly cherish your dedication and high standards!

I want to be very clear: This failure is on us, not on you. You absolutely should keep striving for quality! The reason it wasn't caught isn't because the volunteer improvement was wrong, but because our current production schedule and processes are unstable, and we failed to catch it when we were supposed to.

This brings us back to the production hurdles we discussed yesterday. It’s hard enough for us to reliably screen volunteer translations for mistakes—like the one we found today—when the source text itself is constantly moving.

Today I asked the combat design team again for a concrete schedule on when parts of the Chinese gameplay text will be finalized, but they confirmed it's going to be unpredictable for a while.

So, if we continued with the current** "upload source text → volunteers edit"** route for gameplay text after the lock is lifted:

The source text you see will **likely always be outdated **because we don't have a fast update mechanism to send the text in our Unity files to you guys in real time.

Any **gameplay translation edits you make,** like the ones Mercy was preparing yesterday, are still highly dangerous and might be lost again.

**We absolutely do not want your hard work and time to be wasted because of our internal instability. This is why we need to make a change.**

To protect your effort, we need to temporarily **pause the line-by-line volunteer translation for English gameplay descriptions** after the current translation lock is lifted. 

Since our in-house team is the only one with immediate access to the latest Chinese changes for the combat text, we will handle the **direct implementation of English gameplay translation updates ourselves** for a while.

Your expertise is still essential! We just need to channel it differently. 

Instead of directly translating the unstable gameplay lines, how about we try a "Volunteers give us input on improvement directions, and we handle the implementation according to your feedback" approach? 
You can help us define the terminology, tone, and clarity standards when you see stuff in game that isn't phrased well enough.

Our new in-house member <@1384428399061106739> and our CS <@1131790839291596841> will help me reply to your inquiries when and if I'm not online.

Crucially, all other content is UNAFFECTED:

✅ Story
✅ UI
✅ Panel Text
✅ Anything that doesn't have numbers, basically

These areas aren't going through the massive, unpredictable changes that gameplay text is, so please continue contributing to these areas!

Thank much again for your patience and for being our strongest advocates for quality. We'll set up the new feedback process soon!
could make a channel type like the <#1404748432328888401> type for the "input on improvement directions" so it doesn't get lost in discussion
